title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
problem_statement: Return the kth smallest value in a binary search tree.

examples.explanation: Inorder traversal yields [1,2,3,4], so the smallest element is 1.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 10^4]
intuition: Use inorder traversal to visit BST nodes in sorted order and return the kth visited node.

approaches.name: Inorder traversal
approaches.description: Traverse the tree in order and stop when the kth smallest node is reached.
approaches.why_not: Collecting all values uses extra space and is not necessary if k is small.

interview_qa.a: It visits BST values in ascending order.
Yes, once the kth smallest element is found.
